_comment: Park factors for Atlanta Bandits - VOS Evaluation (UBA)
_comment_2: This file contains park adjustments for evaluating players specifically for the UBA organization
_comment_3: Each team should have its own park factors file (e.g., park-factors-{team-code}.json)

raw_park_factors._comment: Raw park factors from OOTP - 1.0 = league average

park_profile.description: Near-neutral batting average with modest extra-base boost and slight home run suppression. Slightly favors gap hitters over pure power.

tool_adjustments._comment: Multipliers applied to tool ratings. 1.0 = no change, >1.0 = more valuable, <1.0 = less valuable
tool_adjustments._comment_2: These are calculated from raw park factors using conversion formulas

defense._comment: Elevated doubles/triples with slightly depressed average — outfield range still matters for gaps; slightly fewer BIP vs league average
defense._rationale_OFR: Above-average 2B/3B rates reward outfield range on balls in the alleys
defense._rationale_IFR: Slightly below-average average implies marginally fewer balls to infielders

baserunning._comment: Modest doubles/triples environment rewards speed and reads slightly
baserunning._rationale_Run: Triples rate nudges value of smart baserunning

pitcher_ability._comment: Park characteristics affect which pitcher skills are most valuable
pitcher_ability._rationale_Movement: Some help limiting hard contact in the gaps
pitcher_ability._rationale_Control: Marginally more important with slightly depressed average
pitcher_ability._rationale_HR_Avoid: Mild HR suppression — HR avoidance still matters a bit less than in a big HR park

handedness_splits._comment: Optional handedness-specific adjustments - only used if use_handedness_splits is true
handedness_splits._comment_2: RHB 0.960 vs LHB 0.980 HR factors — small split
handedness_splits._rationale: Splits are modest (~2% HR difference) — optional complexity

application_rules._comment: Rules for when and how aggressively to apply these park factors
application_rules._comment_prospects: Apply to all players in Atlanta Bandits / UBA organization, including minors
application_rules._comment_major_leaguers: Apply to players at ML level with this team
application_rules._comment_handedness: Disabled by default — RHB/LHB HR gap is small
application_rules._comment_strength: Use 50% strength as conservative starting point. 1.0 = full strength, 0.5 = half strength
application_rules._comment_strength_2: At 0.5 strength: blended adjustments move halfway toward neutral vs full raw-derived multipliers

validation_examples._comment: Test cases to verify park factors are working correctly

power_hitter_vs_gap_hitter.description: Compare 80 Pow / 45 Gap player vs 45 Pow / 80 Gap player

player_a_power_hitter.impact: Power slightly devalued in mild HR-suppressing park

player_b_gap_hitter.impact: Gap profile gets a small boost from 2B/3B factors

power_hitter_vs_gap_hitter.result: Effects are smaller than extreme parks — tune adjustment_strength if needed

center_fielder_evaluation.description: CF with strong range gets a modest outfield boost

elite_range_cf.with_park_half_strength: Defense score based on ~75 × 1.02 effective OFR (illustrative)
elite_range_cf.impact: Small bump to range contribution in gap-friendly outfield

baserunner_evaluation.description: Smart baserunners get a slight reward

high_baserunning.impact: Mild boost — less dramatic than a spacious triples park

usage_notes.creating_files_for_other_teams: Copy this file and adjust raw_park_factors and team_info sections
usage_notes.tuning_adjustment_strength: Start at 0.5, increase to 0.7-1.0 if you want more aggressive park effects
usage_notes.when_to_enable_handedness_splits: Consider if RHB/LHB HR factors differ by >10% (here ~2%)
